markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority per the operator no-.md directive (KERNEL stub convention).

lifecycle.spec_status: Master Spec 10-product-surfaces (~lines 412/441/444) MANDATES DOCX import/export via AST + a dual RichView+Monaco StructuredTextView. WP-012 deliberately scopes this OUT (export is HTML/MD/TXT/JSON per React parity). This stub carries the spec-mandated scope; refinement reconciles spec vs scope (operator decision).

draft_scope.intent: Carries the spec-mandated DOCX import/export via AST (Master Spec 10-product-surfaces ~lines 412/441/444) and the dual RichView + Monaco StructuredTextView that are deliberately OUT of the WP-KERNEL-012 React-parity export scope (HTML/MD/TXT/JSON). Separates a large AST-fidelity surface from the parity rebuild. This is reconciliation-debt item (b) recorded in the WP-012 refinement: the spec requires DOCX/dual-view but WP-012 excludes it - so it is parked here and MUST NOT be silently dropped; an operator decision is required (build as this WP vs amend the spec).
draft_scope.scope_sketch: IN (subject to refinement): DOCX import/export via a faithful AST round-trip; the dual RichView + structured-text (Monaco-class) view of the same document. OUT: re-doing WP-012 rich-text parity; backend rewrite (typed blockers for gaps); SQLite. Native Rust under src/frontend/**, hosted in the WP-011 shell, reusing handshake_core document + the existing export/ArtifactService contract where present.
key_open_questions: Operator decision: keep DOCX/dual-view as a later WP or expand WP-012 - the spec requires it but WP-012 scope excludes it; do not silently drop.
Which Rust DOCX library reaches the spec AST fidelity bar, and does export route through the existing unified ExportGuard/ArtifactService contract?
Is the dual structured-text view a third editor surface or a projection of the existing WP-012 block document model?
draft_scope.acceptance_criteria: STUB-LEVEL ONLY. Full AC list deferred to refinement. ACTIVATION-AC: refinement records the operator's spec-vs-scope decision, selects the DOCX AST library, defines the dual-view model (third surface vs projection), derives SPEC_ANCHOR/DONE_MEANS, obtains operator signature, and pre-creates the full-detail MT set before execution.

build_order.sequencing_note: Builds on the native rich-text editor + export surface delivered by WP-012. Activate after WP-012 lands; resolves WP-012 reconciliation-debt (b).

spec_trace.roadmap_pointer: Spec-mandated DOCX/dual-view scope (Master Spec 10-product-surfaces ~412/441/444) scoped out of WP-012; parked here pending operator spec-vs-scope decision.

assumptions_to_attack: A stub is planning metadata, not execution authority.
The spec mandates DOCX/dual-view; silently dropping it is a spec-vs-code drift (must be an explicit operator decision).
DOCX export must route through the existing ExportGuard/ArtifactService contract, not a parallel path.
minimum_controls: Keep execution_authority=NON_EXECUTION_STUB until a signed official packet exists.
Refinement must record the operator's keep-as-WP vs amend-spec decision before MT authoring.
Require operator signature before packet creation and worktree preparation.
